sections.paragraph:
i.q. עָלַס and עָלַץ to exult, to rejoice, (originally, I believe, used of a sound of joy, like ἀλαλάζω, רָנַן, not of leaping, dancing, like the syn. גִּיל), 2Sa 1:20 Psa 68:5 followed by בְּ, concerning anything, Psa 149:5 Hab 3:18. It is also applied to inanimate things, Psa 96:12. It is sometimes used in a bad sense, of insolent, haughty men, Psa 94:3 Isa 23:12 compare 5:14 .

Derived noun, עַלִּיז, and-
